nodes.title: Asset Collection
nodes.description: A named group of related assets — e.g. a model railway collection, vinyl record library, or art portfolio. Lets families and dealers understand which items belong together and should be considered as a set

nodes.title: Asset Interest
nodes.description: Captures informal beneficiary interest in an asset or collection — pre-bequest soft preferences. Not legally binding. Helps the testator understand who wants what before making formal bequests

nodes.title: Asset
nodes.description: A non-property asset: financial accounts, personal property, vehicles, digital assets, business interests, Islamic finance instruments, etc

nodes.title: Attestation
nodes.description: Will execution formalities. Standalone — reused by estate, codicils, trusts, extensions. Supports written (E&W Wills Act s.9), oral (Islamic/customary), seal-based (Japanese inkan), kinyan-based (Jewish)

nodes.title: Bequest
nodes.description: A disposition of property or money in a will. Supports 7 core types (specific, pecuniary, demonstrative, general, residuary, life_interest, class) and extension types via extensionType (e.g. wasiyya, halachic_matanah)

nodes.title: INHERIT v1 Catalogue Schema
nodes.description: Lightweight root schema for catalogue-only documents — living collectors cataloguing items without the full estate envelope. Assets, collections, valuations, and legacy contacts. Upgrade path: wrap in a full estate document (schema.json) when needed

nodes.title: Dealer Interest
nodes.description: Level D data — records third-party interest in estate assets (art dealers, property investors, collectors). Privacy-controlled. Managed via proxy authorisation

nodes.title: Document
nodes.description: A document associated with an estate — wills, codicils, trust deeds, and religious documents (shtar_chov, ketubah_document, nikah_nama, etc.). Type is a free string (minLength 1) to accommodate jurisdiction-specific and religious document types

nodes.title: Estate
nodes.description: The root estate record — will, intestacy, or trust-based succession plan. Contains testator details, jurisdiction, will formalities, forced heirship, probate grants, death record, multi-jurisdiction administration, and tax treaty positions

nodes.title: Executor
nodes.description: A person appointed to administer an estate. Includes professional executors, administrators (intestacy), and administrators with will annexed

nodes.title: Guardian
nodes.description: A guardian appointment for a minor child. Supports testamentary, court-appointed, shariah court, community-appointed, and religious court appointments. Guardianship structures include individual, collective, rotating, and family council determined

nodes.title: Kinship
nodes.description: A familial bond between two people. Essential for intestacy calculation — inheritance rules in every jurisdiction depend on parent-child, sibling, and other familial relationships. Direction: fromPersonId is the parent/elder, toPersonId is the child/younger (for parent-child types). Indian extensions may add PARENT_CHILD_COPARCENARY, KARTA_MEMBER. See extensions/hindu-succession/hindu-succession.json

nodes.title: Liability
nodes.description: A debt or financial obligation. Includes mortgages, loans, credit cards, and cultural obligations (mahr, ketubah debt, lobola) that are priority debts in certain jurisdictions

nodes.title: Lifetime Transfer
nodes.description: A gift or transfer made during the testator's lifetime, relevant for tax calculations such as the UK 7-year rule, US unified gift/estate tax, and similar lookback provisions in other jurisdictions

nodes.title: Nonprobate Transfer
nodes.description: Assets that pass outside the probate estate — revocable trusts, TOD deeds, POD accounts, JTWROS, beneficiary designations, superannuation/CPF/EPF nominations

nodes.title: Person
nodes.description: A person involved in the estate. Schema.org name alignment. Any Unicode script. Supports CJK phonetic readings, chieftaincy titles, clan/lineage identifiers, dual legal personalities, and tax residency across multiple jurisdictions

nodes.title: Property
nodes.description: Real estate. Supports individual ownership, communal/family property (Nigeria), HUF coparcenary (India), government-vested land (Land Use Act), US community property, and informal/unregistered holdings

nodes.title: Proxy Authorisation
nodes.description: Authorises a proxy to act on behalf of a testator — information gathering, communication, negotiation, or full decision-making. Includes consent record with cultural consent methods

nodes.title: Relationship
nodes.description: Spousal/partnership relationship. N-partner support (no structural two-partner limit). Event-based timeline (status derived from events). No gendered role names. Financial instruments with MAHR/KETUBAH subtype validation. Cross-jurisdiction recognition for dual civil/religious status

nodes.title: INHERIT v1 Root Schema
nodes.description: Root entry point for an INHERIT v1 estate data interchange document. Contains a single estate and arrays of all entity types

nodes.title: Trust
nodes.description: A trust instrument — testamentary or inter vivos. Covers discretionary, bare, life interest, charitable, nil rate band, waqf (Islamic endowment), and offshore structures with governance features (reserved powers, flee clauses, protector powers)

nodes.title: Valuation
nodes.description: A valuation of an estate asset, property, or asset collection. Supports multiple valuations per entity over time — owner estimates, professional valuations, AI analyses, dealer offers, and probate-agreed figures

nodes.title: Wish
nodes.description: A non-binding (or culturally/religiously binding) wish — funeral, letter of wishes, care instructions, pet care, digital estate, distribution preferences

nodes.title: PostalAddress
nodes.description: A physical address. Culturally neutral — supports any global addressing convention

nodes.title: Completeness
nodes.description: A completeness score derived from a weighted checklist of estate data categories, scoped to a specific jurisdiction and estate status

nodes.title: Identifier
nodes.description: A typed external identifier. Use 'system' to namespace identifiers and prevent collisions across jurisdictions and organisations

nodes.title: Jurisdiction
nodes.description: A legal jurisdiction identified by ISO 3166 codes. Succession law varies not just by country but by subdivision — Scotland differs from England and Wales, and US states have radically different rules

nodes.title: Media
nodes.description: A media attachment — photograph, video, or document scan. Used for identification, valuation, condition documentation, and provenance records

nodes.title: Money
nodes.description: Monetary value in integer minor currency units with ISO 4217 code. Never floating point

nodes.title: TaxPosition
nodes.description: An estimated tax position for an estate in a specific jurisdiction, including gross and net values, available exemptions, and estimated liability

nodes.title: TemporalRule
nodes.description: A jurisdiction rule value with effective dates, legislative status, and change history

nodes.title: Visibility
nodes.description: Reusable visibility level controlling which parties can see an entity. Used across dealer interests, asset interests, and other privacy-sensitive records
